cards.back: Inter-Process Communication (IPC) methods are techniques used by processes to communicate with each other. Examples include pipes, FIFOs, System V IPC, message queues, and semaphores.

cards.front: What is the purpose of popen?
cards.back: popen is a function that opens a process to execute a command as if the command were executed directly from the shell. It is used to run shell commands in a separate process.

cards.front: What is the purpose of pclose?
cards.back: pclose is a function that closes and terminates the process opened by popen. It is used to close the process and free up system resources.
A coprocess is a special type of process that is created by a process and is used for communication with the parent process or other processes.
A FIFO (First-In-First-Out) is a type of inter-process communication that allows one process to send data to another process, and the data is processed in the order it was received.
